fields.label: Notification Method

fields.description: <small>Receive email when customers fulfill payment requests.</small>
fields.label: Email Notification

fields.description: When enabled, Stripe Plus will override the original checkout page for Stripe.
fields.label: Enable Stripe Plus
Hide warning in Payment Request when Stripe Plus is disabled

fields.description: <small>Connecting the webhook receiver with your Stripe account unlocks accounting automation (automatic payment entry and payout journal entry) and more features such as notifications and events logging.</small>
fields.label: Set Up
Instructions
fields.options:
Instructions
  1. Go to your Stripe dashboard. Navigate to the bottom bar and look for 'Developers' menu button.
  2. Click the button and choose 'Webhooks'. Wait until a pane appears.
  3. From the pane, click the 'Add destination' button.
  4. Select all events related to Charge, Payment Intent, Setup Intent and Payout, then continue.
      5. Optional: Also select Invoice and Customer.Subscription events if you intend to use Stripe Plus Subscription.
  5. Choose the destination type to 'Webhook endpoint', then continue.
  6. Choose a destination name and set the endpoint url to https://{your_domain}/api/method/erpusa.stripe_plus.api.webhook_receiver.receive_stripe_events .
  7. Finally, create the destination.
  8. Once the destination is created, look for its signing secret and copy it.
  9. In the table below, add a row where the Stripe Settings is the account you used and the Signing Secret is the text you copied.

fields.description: <small>There can be up to 24 timeslots and each timeslot can have hour and minute.</small>
<small>Because Accounting data is sensitive, an admin must authorize the creation of Payment Entry. Leaving this empty won't automatically create a Payment Entry.</small>
fields.label: User to Authorize Payment Entry Creation (must be an Accounts Manager or someone with a higher position)

fields.description:
The cost center in the Payment Entry is determined by the item with the highest amount in the invoice. If a cost center can’t be determined, the value in this field will be applied.
The default merchant fee account and cost center will also be used for stripe fee in journal entries.
fields.label: Default Merchant Fee Cost Center

fields.description: <small>This setting is only applicable when payment is applied to Sales Invoice (including automatically generated invoices via Payment Requests). Subscription Invoices are always submitted.</small>
fields.label: Automatically submit Payment Entry
ERP-Stripe Accounts (Required to automatically create payout journal entry)
Notes
fields.options:
Notes
  1. Stripe Plus only supports payment methods allowed in the US. During creation of Payment Method Configuration, some payment methods will not appear.
  2. To make advanced features work, the website should be public.
  3. It is best practice to have a dedicated Stripe account for a site. If two websites share the same Stripe account, there can be unexpected results.

fields.description: For multiple addresses, enter the address on different line. e.g. test@test.com ⏎ test1@test.com
fields.label: Notification Recipients
Add new checkout customers to Stripe.com account

fields.description: When disabled, the error will be  logged instead.
fields.label: Show error through a pop up window if customer adding fails

fields.description: If empty, defaults to: Request to Subscribe and Initiate Payment via Stripe
fields.label: Email Subject
Email Content Instructions
fields.options:
Subscription info can be inserted in the email subject and message. To insert a subscription info, write the info code enclosed in parentheses, like so: {{ name }}. The list below enumerates all the supported Subscription Info.

Instructions
  1. name. The name of the subscription.
  2. customer
  3. company
  4. start_date. The date when the subscription will begin.
  5. end_date. The date when the subscription will end.
  6. subscription_plan_table. A tabulation of the subscription plans included. (email message only)
  7. payment_url. The link where the customer can pay. (email message only, mandatory)

fields.label: Subscription Notes
fields.options: A set-up Advanced Features is required to enable Subscription as it keeps track of customer payments to automatically make Payment Entries against Sales Invoices.
Maximum Number of Payment Method Changes (X times in X minutes)

fields.label: Default Recipient of Subscription Update Emails
Card Expiry Notification Lead Time (in Months)
Card Expiry Notification Repeat
fields.options:
Notify Once

fields.description:
When enabled, Sales Invoices generated from Subscriptions will automatically have their Allocate Advances Automatically field checked.
Suitable for situations where the payment gets processed before the invoice is created.
fields.label: Automatically Apply Advance Payments

fields.description: <small>The credit account for each Journal Entry item is determined by the payment account in the referenced Payment Request or Subscription. If a credit account can't be determined, the value in this field will be applied.</small>
fields.label: Default Credit Account
